title: pavonia hastata
intro: Pavonia hastata, also known as swamp rose-mallow, is a perennial plant species belonging to the hibiscus family (Malvaceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating skin conditions such as eczema, acne, and wounds due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant's roots, leaves, and flowers are used in herbal preparations, which have been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, Pavonia hastata is often combined with other herbs to create teas, tinctures, and salves that are used to promote wound healing, soothe skin irritations, and support overall well-being.
